GA4 Certification Micro-Applications

Author

Ari Nurhadi

Published

March 13, 2026

Certification Proof

Exam Reflection

Results Summary

I passed the Google Analytics Certification exam with a final score of 96%. The exam evaluated my understanding of GA4 measurement concepts, reporting tools, explorations, and integration’s with other Google platforms. The questions required applying GA4 concepts to real analytics scenarios, including analyzing user journeys, identifying conversion opportunities, and understanding how GA4 data supports marketing decisions.

Top 3 Topics That Were Most Challenging

  • Understanding when to use different exploration types such as funnel exploration, path exploration, and segment overlap analysis.

  • Interpreting attribution models and how different models affect conversion credit across multiple marketing channels.

  • Understanding how GA4 integration’s (Google Ads, BigQuery, and Looker Studio) extend analysis and activation capabilities.

Two Questions I Wish I Could Redo

One concept I would revisit involves choosing the correct exploration technique to analyze specific business questions. While I understand the purpose of each exploration type, some exam scenarios required carefully matching the business problem with the most appropriate exploration method. Another concept I would revisit involves attribution models and identifying when data-driven attribution is preferred over rule-based models.

CEP Connection

Because of what I learned from the certification exam, I will improve my CEP measurement plan by focusing more on funnel-based analysis of the conversion journey. Specifically, I will monitor drop-offs between key events such as product views, add-to-cart actions, and purchases. I will also create GA4 audiences based on high-intent behaviors to support remarketing strategies and improve overall conversion performance.

GA4 Readiness Check

KPI

Purchase Conversion Rate – the percentage of users who complete a purchase after starting a session.

Supporting Exploration

Funnel Exploration tracking the path from session_start to purchase.

Explanation

The primary KPI is purchase conversion rate. This funnel exploration tracks the user journey from session_start to purchase and highlights where users drop off in the buying process. By analyzing the largest drop-offs (such as between view_item and add_to_cart), improvements can be made to product pages and checkout flows to increase overall conversion performance.
